title: In conversation with Sheryl Sandberg, plus open-source AI gene editing explained
description:
(0:00) Welcoming Sheryl Sandberg and remembering Dave Goldberg

(11:10) What led Sheryl to get involved with "Screams Before Silence"; reaction to sexual violence on and after 10/7

(28:18) Paths forward, documentary decisions, involvement of women in protests

(53:03) Post-interview debrief

(59:45) Science Corner: Open-source AI gene editing with OpenCRISPR-1

analysis.summary: The episode features a conversation with Sheryl Sandberg about her involvement with 'Screams Before Silence' and her reaction to sexual violence. The hosts also discuss open-source AI gene editing with OpenCRISPR-1 in the Science Corner segment. The conversation covers various topics including women's involvement in protests and documentary decisions.

takeaways.text: Sheryl Sandberg is involved with 'Screams Before Silence'
OpenCRISPR-1 is an open-source AI gene editing tool
Women's involvement in protests is crucial for creating change
Documentary decisions can impact the narrative and message
AI gene editing has the potential to revolutionize various fields
Sexual violence is a serious issue that needs to be addressed
Open-source AI tools can accelerate innovation and progress
Collaboration and awareness are key to creating positive change

quotes.text: Something we can all agree on
quotes.speaker: Sheryl Sandberg

sentiment.notes: The conversation covers serious topics like sexual violence, but also highlights the potential for positive change and innovation through open-source AI tools and collaboration.

notable_moments.description: Introduction to Sheryl Sandberg and the topic of 'Screams Before Silence'
Introduction to the Science Corner segment on open-source AI gene editing with OpenCRISPR-1
